summary: Implementation plan for the 1985-NY-Yankees Azure Function project. An Azure Timer-triggered Function (Python) queries GPT-4o via TRAPI nightly, retrieves the 1985 New York Yankees roster, and persists it to Azure Blob Storage. Authentication is managed entirely via Managed Identity (no API keys). Infrastructure is provisioned with Bicep on Azure Consumption Plan.
assumptions: Python is the implementation language based on existing repo conventions (trapi_client.py, requirements.txt).
Azure Functions Consumption Plan (Linux) is the target runtime, consistent with repo memory.
Bicep is the IaC toolchain, consistent with repo memory showing /infra with main.bicep.
TRAPI endpoint and GPT-4o model deployment are accessible via Managed Identity; no API key is required per NFR.
GitHub Actions is the CI/CD pipeline.
Blob container name is 'yankees-roster' (consistent with existing infra memory).
Timer schedule is nightly at midnight UTC (0 0 * * *).
Output blob is a JSON file containing the player list.
Application Insights is used for observability, provisioned via existing or new Bicep module.
The plan is NOT automatically approved; manual review is required.
open_questions: What is the exact TRAPI endpoint URL and GPT-4o deployment name?
What is the expected blob output format (JSON array, CSV, newline-delimited)?
Should the nightly run overwrite a single blob or append/version-stamp each run?
Are there retry or alerting SLAs defined (e.g., page on failure)?
Is there an existing Application Insights instance to reuse, or must one be created?
What Azure subscription/resource group should be targeted for deployment?
Are there cost constraints on the Consumption Plan or storage tier?
Should the function skip or overwrite if the blob already exists for the day?

waves.name: Foundation
waves.objective: Establish infrastructure, managed identity, RBAC, and CI/CD pipeline so all feature work has a deployable target.
entry_criteria: spec.md reviewed and implementation task plan approved (PROJ-01)
Azure subscription and resource group available
exit_criteria: Bicep infrastructure deploys cleanly with no errors
Managed Identity exists and RBAC roles are assigned
CI/CD pipeline runs and deploys a stub function successfully
Application Insights connected to Function App

waves.name: Core Build
waves.objective: Implement the TRAPI/GPT-4o player retrieval, blob storage writer, and the Timer-triggered Azure Function that orchestrates them nightly.
exit_criteria: TRAPI client returns 1985 Yankees roster from GPT-4o
Blob writer persists roster to 'yankees-roster' container
Timer trigger fires on schedule and end-to-end flow succeeds

waves.name: Hardening
waves.objective: Add reliability, error handling, observability, and security controls to make the function production-ready.
entry_criteria: Core Build wave exit criteria met
End-to-end happy path verified in dev/staging
exit_criteria: Structured logging and Application Insights telemetry confirmed
Alert rules active for function failures
No secrets or API keys in code or config

waves.name: Release Readiness
waves.objective: Validate production deployment, confirm monitoring is active, complete documentation, and sign off on all acceptance criteria.
exit_criteria: Function deployed to production and nightly execution confirmed
Blob contains valid 1985 Yankees roster after first real run
All spec acceptance criteria checked off

tasks.title: Review and approve implementation task plan
tasks.description: Review this implementation task plan for completeness, correctness, and alignment with the spec. Approve before any engineering work begins. Resolve all open questions if possible.
outputs: Resolved open questions logged in GitHub issue
acceptance_criteria: Task plan reviewed by rciapala or designated tech lead
All open questions either resolved or explicitly deferred with rationale
Task plan approved and engineering work authorized to begin
GitHub issue created and labeled 'start-assign'
risks: Unresolved open questions (TRAPI endpoint, output format) could block INF-01 and APP-01

tasks.title: Provision core Azure infrastructure via Bicep
tasks.description: Author or update Bicep modules under /infra to provision: Azure Storage Account with 'yankees-roster' blob container, Linux Consumption Function App (Python 3.11), System-assigned Managed Identity, and Application Insights. Use identity-based storage connection (AzureWebJobsStorage__accountName) with no storage keys.
outputs: Deployed resource group with all required Azure resources
acceptance_criteria: `az deployment group create` on main.bicep completes with no errors
Function App exists with System-assigned Managed Identity enabled
Storage Account and 'yankees-roster' blob container exist
Application Insights resource connected to Function App (APPLICATIONINSIGHTS_CONNECTION_STRING set)
AzureWebJobsStorage__accountName is set; no storage access keys used
Resources tagged with project=1985-NY-Yankees and owner=rciapala
PR created with @copilot assigned for review and merged
risks: Subscription quota limits for Consumption Plan in target region
Existing /infra modules may need structural changes to add Application Insights

tasks.title: Configure Managed Identity RBAC grants for Storage and TRAPI
tasks.description: Assign 'Storage Blob Data Contributor' RBAC role to the Function App's Managed Identity on the 'yankees-roster' container. Confirm that TRAPI/GPT-4o access uses Managed Identity (Entra token) and requires no API key, per the security NFR.
outputs: TRAPI auth pattern confirmed and documented
acceptance_criteria: Managed Identity has 'Storage Blob Data Contributor' on the yankees-roster container (verified via `az role assignment list`)
No storage account keys or SAS tokens in app settings or code
TRAPI authentication uses Entra-issued token obtained via DefaultAzureCredential; confirmed no API key in config
RBAC grants defined in Bicep (not manual portal assignments)
PR created with @copilot assigned for review and merged
risks: TRAPI may require specific Entra app registration or scope that is not yet documented
Propagation delay for new RBAC assignments can cause transient test failures

tasks.title: Set up GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline for Function App deployment
tasks.description: Create a GitHub Actions workflow (.github/workflows/deploy.yml) that: runs pytest on PRs, and deploys the Azure Function to the Consumption Plan on merge to main using OIDC federated identity (no stored credentials).
inputs: INF-01 outputs (Function App name, resource group)
outputs: OIDC federated credential configured in Azure
acceptance_criteria: GitHub Actions workflow triggers on push to main and on PRs
Pytest step runs and fails the pipeline if tests fail
Deployment step uses OIDC (no client secret stored in GitHub secrets)
Successful deployment verified by checking Function App version after merge
PR created with @copilot assigned for review and merged
risks: OIDC federated credential setup requires Azure AD app registration and may need admin consent
Function App cold-start after fresh deployment may delay initial health check

tasks.title: Implement TRAPI client to query GPT-4o for 1985 Yankees roster
tasks.description: Implement or extend trapi_client.py to query GPT-4o via TRAPI using DefaultAzureCredential (Managed Identity in production, interactive/env-based locally). The prompt must ask for the full roster of the 1985 New York Yankees and return a structured list of player names.
inputs: TRAPI endpoint URL and GPT-4o deployment name (from open questions)
acceptance_criteria: get_1985_yankees_roster() returns a non-empty list of player name strings
Authentication uses DefaultAzureCredential only; no API keys or hardcoded tokens
Unit tests mock the TRAPI HTTP call and assert correct parsing of GPT-4o response
All existing and new tests pass: `python -m pytest tests/ -v --cov=trapi_client --cov-report=term-missing` (link test results in PR)
Coverage for trapi_client.py >= 80%
PR created with @copilot assigned for review and merged
risks: GPT-4o response format may be non-deterministic; prompt engineering required for reliable structured output
TRAPI endpoint details unresolved (open question)

tasks.title: Implement Azure Blob Storage writer for player roster
tasks.description: Implement blob_writer.py with a write_roster_to_blob() function that serializes the player list as JSON and writes it to the 'yankees-roster' container using azure-storage-blob SDK with DefaultAzureCredential. Each run should overwrite a fixed blob name (e.g., roster.json) or use a datestamped name if versioning is required.
acceptance_criteria: write_roster_to_blob() uploads a valid JSON blob to 'yankees-roster' container
Authentication uses DefaultAzureCredential only; no connection strings or keys
Unit tests mock the BlobServiceClient and assert correct blob upload call
All existing and new tests pass: `python -m pytest tests/ -v --cov=blob_writer --cov-report=term-missing` (link test results in PR)
Coverage for blob_writer.py >= 80%
PR created with @copilot assigned for review and merged
risks: RBAC propagation delay may cause 403 errors in integration tests run immediately after INF-01
Blob overwrite vs. versioning strategy needs decision (open question)

tasks.title: Implement nightly Timer-triggered Azure Function
tasks.description: Implement the Azure Function (function_app.py or equivalent) with a Timer trigger (NCRONTAB: '0 0 * * *') that calls get_1985_yankees_roster() and write_roster_to_blob() in sequence. Include structured logging at each step.
inputs: Azure Functions Python v2 programming model docs
acceptance_criteria: Timer trigger NCRONTAB schedule is '0 0 * * *' (midnight UTC daily)
Function calls TRAPI client and blob writer in sequence; errors are caught and logged
Structured logs emitted for: function start, roster retrieved (with count), blob write success/failure
Unit tests mock dependencies and assert orchestration logic
All tests pass: `python -m pytest tests/ -v --cov=function_app --cov-report=term-missing` (link test results in PR)
PR created with @copilot assigned for review and merged
risks: Timer trigger may not fire at exact midnight due to cold start on Consumption Plan
Dependency on both APP-01 and APP-02 makes this the integration point; delays upstream block this task

tasks.title: Configure Application Insights structured logging and alerting
tasks.description: Ensure the Function App emits structured telemetry to Application Insights. Create an Azure Monitor alert rule that fires when the function reports a failure (exceptions or result != success) so the team is notified of missed nightly runs.
outputs: Alert rule in Bicep or ARM template
Verified Application Insights live metrics showing function executions
acceptance_criteria: Function executions appear in Application Insights under 'Live Metrics' and 'Failures' blade
Alert rule fires when function execution result is 'Failed' (verified by triggering a deliberate failure in staging)
Alert notifications routed to at least one notification channel (email or webhook)
Alert rule defined as code (Bicep/ARM) not manual portal config
PR created with @copilot assigned for review and merged
risks: Alert channel (email/webhook target) not defined in spec; requires decision
Consumption Plan cold starts may generate spurious timeout alerts on first nightly run

tasks.title: Implement retry and error handling for TRAPI and Blob operations
tasks.description: Add retry logic with exponential backoff to TRAPI calls (transient HTTP errors) and blob write operations. Ensure the function logs a structured error and does not silently swallow exceptions. Define a maximum retry count to avoid infinite loops.
outputs: Updated trapi_client.py and blob_writer.py with retry logic
Updated unit tests covering retry paths
acceptance_criteria: TRAPI client retries up to 3 times on transient errors (5xx, timeout) with exponential backoff
Blob writer retries up to 3 times on transient errors
After exhausting retries, exception is re-raised so the Function runtime marks the execution as failed
Unit tests assert retry behavior using mocked HTTP responses
All tests pass (link results in PR)
PR created with @copilot assigned for review and merged
risks: Overly aggressive retry may cause GPT-4o quota exhaustion if TRAPI has per-minute rate limits

tasks.title: Security review: validate no secrets in code or config
tasks.description: Perform a security review of all code and configuration to confirm no API keys, connection strings, passwords, or tokens are present. Validate that DefaultAzureCredential is used exclusively and that local.settings.json is git-ignored.
outputs: Any remediation PRs if secrets found
acceptance_criteria: GitHub secret scanning shows zero detected secrets in the repository
local.settings.json is listed in .gitignore
No API keys, SAS tokens, or connection strings appear in any committed file (grep confirms)
DefaultAzureCredential used in all Azure SDK calls (grep confirms no hardcoded credential classes)
Review sign-off recorded in GitHub issue
risks: Developer tooling (local.settings.json) may be accidentally committed if .gitignore is misconfigured

tasks.title: End-to-end integration test and production deployment validation
tasks.description: Deploy to production and manually trigger the function (or wait for the nightly run). Verify the 'yankees-roster' blob is created with a valid JSON list of 1985 Yankees players. Confirm Application Insights shows a successful execution.
inputs: Azure Storage Explorer or az cli
outputs: Confirmed blob contents screenshot/artifact in results/
acceptance_criteria: Function manually triggered or nightly run observed in Application Insights
Blob 'roster.json' (or datestamped equivalent) exists in 'yankees-roster' container
Blob content is valid JSON array of player name strings (validated with az storage blob download)
Application Insights shows zero failures for the execution
Test results artifact stored in results/ directory in the repository
risks: Nightly timer makes same-day validation require a manual trigger
TRAPI/GPT-4o availability in production environment not yet confirmed

tasks.title: Write README and operational runbook
tasks.description: Write a README.md covering: project purpose, architecture overview, local development setup, deployment steps, and how to manually trigger the function. Write an ops runbook covering: how to check the nightly run, how to re-run on failure, and how to interpret Application Insights alerts.
acceptance_criteria: README.md includes: project description, architecture diagram or description, local dev setup, deployment instructions
Runbook includes: how to verify nightly run, re-run steps, alert response procedures
All commands in README are tested and work on a clean checkout
Documentation PR reviewed and merged
risks: Documentation may become stale if written before all implementation decisions are finalized
